indicators_measured: food security score, dietary diversity score, intercropping rate, use of organic amendments (compost/manure)
strengths_and_limitations: Strength: Longitudinal design allows for tracking changes over time. Limitation: Attrition between baseline and follow-up surveys.
lessons_learned: The study highlights the importance of participatory research approaches that promote farmer experimentation and gender equity for improving food security and nutrition.
validity_notes: Attrition between baseline (n=306) and follow-up surveys (n=352), small sample size at end of study period (n=203 matched households).

adaptation_focus: indicator-based framework for assessing adaptive capacity
indicators_measured: economic resources, social capital, awareness and training, technology, infrastructure, institutions
strengths_and_limitations: Strength: Indicator-based framework provides a structured approach to assessing adaptive capacity. Limitation: Limited to two agrarian communities, may not be generalizable.
lessons_learned: The study recommends resilience building interventions targeting individuals with low adaptive capacities, especially women and farmers without formal education.
validity_notes: small sample (n < 30), single-site study, self-reported outcomes

adaptation_focus: farmers' attitudes and determinants of adaptation to climate risks
process_outcome_domains: knowledge_awareness_learning; uptake_adoption; participation_coproduction; access_information_services; yields_productivity; income_assets
indicators_measured: adaptation index, household size, sex of the household head, number of months of food shortage, access to credit

adaptation_focus: climate change vulnerability assessment for smallholder agricultural livelihoods
strengths_and_limitations: Strength: Comprehensive analysis at municipality level. Limitation: Limited to four countries and specific crops.

adaptation_focus: autonomous adaptation strategies (seasonal calendar adjustment, variety/breed tolerance, integrated crop models, income diversification)
indicators_measured: adoption rate (%), number of available information sources, number of workers on the farm, farmable land during summer season
strengths_and_limitations: Strength: Used mixed-method approach including interviews and focus groups. Limitation: Single cross-sectional study, limited to one province.
validity_notes: small sample (n=127), single-site study, self-reported outcomes
